verses.1: A Psalm, a Song for the Sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to the LORD And to sing praises to Your name, Most High;
verses.2: To declare Your goodness in the morning And Your faithfulness by night,
verses.3: With the ten-stringed lute and with the harp, With resounding music on the lyre.
verses.4: For You, LORD, have made me joyful by what You have done, I will sing for joy over the works of Your hands.
verses.5: How great are Your works, LORD! Your thoughts are very deep.
verses.6: A stupid person has no knowledge, Nor does a foolish person understand this:
verses.7: When the wicked sprouted up like grass And all who did injustice flourished, It was only that they might be destroyed forevermore.
verses.8: But You, LORD, are on high forever.
verses.9: For, behold, Your enemies, LORD, For, behold, Your enemies will perish; All who do injustice will be scattered.
verses.10: But You have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox; I have been anointed with fresh oil.
verses.11: And my eye has looked at my enemies, My ears hear of the evildoers who rise up against me.
verses.12: The righteous person will flourish like the palm tree, He will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
verses.13: Planted in the house of the LORD, They will flourish in the courtyards of our God.
verses.14: They will still yield fruit in advanced age; They will be full of sap and very green,
verses.15: To declare that the LORD is just; He is my rock, and there is no malice in Him.
